Qoute

Start where you are, learn what you can, and keep building—every line of code today shapes the software and the person you’ll become tomorrow.

About

I first learned how to program when I was in Grade 9, when I took programming as an elective subject. That was my introduction to coding, and the first languages I learned was Java, JavaScript, and Python. It helped me understand the basics of programming such as logic, problem solving, and how software works. As I continued my studies, I kept my interest in programming and brought it with me into college. I am currently a second-year college student at the University of Northern Philippines, where I continue to develop my programming skills. Over time I started exploring more technologies and expanding beyond the language I first learned. Right now I am learning multiple programming languages, including Go, and I am also re-learning HTML and CSS to strengthen my understanding of web development. Programming has become an important part of my academic life and something I continue to improve as I study and gain more experience.
